finding: Bank willingness stable but economic outlook weighs on Slovak firms
bullets: A net 7.8% of Slovak firms (n=310) reported improved bank willingness to lend, unchanged from wave 38 and above both the EA's net 5.6% and Germany's net -4.2% — suggesting stable credit supply conditions.

The general economic outlook deteriorated for a net 51.6% of Slovak firms (n=472), worsening by 7.2 pp from wave 38's net 44.4% — 22.2 pp more adverse than the EA's net 29.4%, putting pressure on financing access.
Access to public financial support improved by 12.6 pp to a net 21.2% of firms reporting deterioration (n=310), compared with a net 33.8% in wave 38, though conditions remain negative.
chart_subtitle: Net 7.8% of Slovak firms report improved bank lending willingness.
